title: Glass app disconnects during use
description:
During a ride, sometimes the Glass app disconnects from the phone app even though the two are still Bluetooth paired. It does not reconnect, and it is not clear to the user that there is a problem.

Ideally, the app would never disconnect. Investiate and fix sources of connection instability.

IF the Glass app does lose its connection, it should try to reestablish the connection. If it cannot, it should wake up the screen and show a "DISCONNECTED" message in large text until dismissed.

It is acceptable to modify both apps to resolve this issue.
close_reason: Added application-level Keepalive (5s ping, 15s watchdog) to RFCOMM + TCP transports on both sides; closes the socket on silent BT drops so the existing reconnect path runs. On Glass, transport disconnect now triggers a 10s-grace screen-wake alert with large 'DISCONNECTED' text on the LiveCard, cleared on reconnect. 6 new Keepalive unit tests + existing TcpTransport roundtrip pass.

title: Use speed-based pre-turn wakeups
description:
There is currently a wakeup 150m before the next turn. That's about right for cycling on average, but too close if cycling fast, and not ideal for driving or walking. Instead, there should be a minimum, plus extra distance based on current speed. Change this to:

Walking: 30m
Cycling: 150m
Driving: 500m

Or 0.7m per km/h, whichever is greater.

title: Toggle to orinet map in the direction of travel
description: Add a setting in the phone app to switch between north-up and travel-direction-up. This should apply to the map sent to the Glass as well.

title: Map is cluttered
description:
The map shown on both the phone and Glass displays contains a large amount of clutter, making it difficulty to recognize the roads and route at a glance.

Investigate options to declutter the map. If there's a straightforward obvious option, implement it. If not, create a follow up issue with options.

Increase the contrast of the map if possible.
